Row.i__Decommissioning_plan_to_reflect_the_p: The GHG emissions projection is calculated based on the following assumptions for each respective source of emission:

a. Production, feedgas and CO2 mol% : figures sourced from P4R submission for MLNG as of 21st Oct 2024 (for Y2025-2029) and Long Term PLC Production Run KPBI for Y2030. Discrepancy between projected CO2% vs actual CO2% is expected.
b. Stationary combustion and fugitive emission: 10.16% of feedgas (mmbtu) is used as FG and FE (as per plant PTE; average 89%).

c. Flaring: Operational flaring (only for MCHE tube leak at MLNG DUA, SVR flaring and equipment trip) + SDSU flaring (planned and unplanned) + Module trip flaring (Estimated frequency as per historical FY2021-2023).
d. Indirect emission: SEB power import (20 MW for 2025, increasing to 90 MW from 2026 onwards).
e. Mobile Transportation: Based on average GHG 2021 -2023 + Taking account of EV car implementation.
f. Traces of hydrocarbon in sour vent gas: Based on actual composition of traces of hydrocarbon in sour vent gas (sampling done in 2024)
Slugcatcher 1/2/3+ Bstab 5/6 are not included in the GHG calculation.

New MCHE have been installed in Module 5 in Y2022 to resolve MCHE tube leak issue. Module 4 is planned to replace with new MCHE in Y2025 whereas Mod 6 is planned for MCHE replacement in the subsequent TA window (may be subject to change). Operational Flaring is projected to reduce further post 2025.
45 EV cars will be replacing existing vehicles in PLC by 2024, reducing 101.6 tCo2e/year by 2025.

Glory 1 is projected to import 90MW from Y2026 onwards, this shall be reflected on the GHG reduction of 0.5 MtCO2e/year. However, following the commissioning of GLORY 1, a reduction in power import from SEB to MLNG is anticipated until the commissioning of Baleh Hydropower in 2029.
